name: Basic Crepes
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: Southern Living
Preparation Time: 0:20
1. In a large bowl, whisk together the all-purpose flour and salt.
2. Gradually add milk to the flour mixture, whisking constantly until smooth.
3. Add eggs one at a time, stirring continuously after each addition.
4. Stir in the melted butter until well combined.
5. Cover the batter and refrigerate for 1 hour to allow the flour particles to swell and soften, resulting in light crepes.
6. Heat a 6-inch crepe pan or heavy skillet over medium heat. Brush the bottom lightly with vegetable oil.
7. Pour about 2 tablespoons of batter into the center of the hot pan.
8. Tilt the pan to spread the batter evenly across its surface, forming a thin layer.
9. Cook for about 1 minute until the edges start to lift from the pan and become slightly set.
10. Test doneness by lifting an edge; if it easily releases, proceed to flip the crepe.
11. Flip the crepe carefully and cook on the other side for another 30 seconds.
12. Remove the cooked crepe from the pan using a spatula and place it on a clean towel to cool.
13. Repeat steps 7-12 until all the batter is used, stacking crepes with layers of wax paper between each to prevent sticking.
14. Store leftover crepes in an airtight container or freezer bag for up to one month.
